zh.lifeSummary: John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick是Guillaume Vervolière和N的兒子，出生在英國奧可福爾胡可 諾頓。他於1234年與Christian Sanford在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1223年登記服兵役。他於1241年在英國維爾爾庫貝 比瑟特被列為guardian of heiresses。他去世於1263年2月26日在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國巴金哈姆爾格亞特 米森登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick是Guillaume Vervolière和N的儿子，出生在英国奧可福爾胡可 諾頓。他于1234年与Christian Sanford在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1223年登记服兵役。他于1241年在英国維爾爾庫貝 比瑟特被列为guardian of heiresses。他去世于1263年2月26日在他的家乡，埋葬在英国巴金哈姆爾格亞特 米森登。

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick was born in Hook Norton, Oxfordshire, England as the son of Guillaume Vervolière and N. He married Christian Sanford in 1234, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He registered for military service in 1223. In 1241, his occupation is listed as guardian of heiresses in Coombe Bissett, Wiltshire, England. He died on 26 February 1263, in his hometown, and was buried in Great Missenden, Buckinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick est né à Hook Norton, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, fils de Guillaume Vervolière et N. Il a épousé Christian Sanford en 1234, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1223. En 1241, sa profession de son indiquée est guardian of heiresses à Coombe Bissett, Wiltshire, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 26 février 1263, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Great Missenden, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick wurde in Hook Norton, Oxfordshire, England, als Sohn von Guillaume Vervolière und N geboren. Er hat sich 1223 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1241 ist sein Beruf als guardian of heiresses in Coombe Bissett, Wiltshire, England aufgeführt. Er starb am 26. Februar 1263 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Great Missenden, Buckinghamshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick è nato il nel Hook Norton, Oxfordshire, England come figlio di Guillaume Vervolière e N. Lui sposato Christian Sanford nel 1234, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1223. Nel 1241, la sua occupazione è indicata come guardian of heiresses nel Coombe Bissett, Wiltshire, England. Morì il 26 febbraio 1263, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Great Missenden, Buckinghamshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwickはイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアホーオク ノートンでGuillaume VervolièreとNの息子として生まれた。1234年にイギリスイギリスでChristian Sanfordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1223年に兵役登録をした。1241年，イギリスウィルトシュアイアコオンベ ビセットでの職業はguardian of heiressesと記録されている。彼は1263年2月26日に故郷で死去し，イギリスブクインガムシュアイアグレアト ミセンデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick(은)는 영국 스퍼드실르 훅 노르턴에서 Guillaume Vervolière(와)과 N의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1234년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Christian Sanford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1223년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1241년, 그의 직업은 영국 윌츠히르 쿠브 비싯에서 guardian of heiresses이었다. 그는 1263년 2월 26일 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 벅잉함실르 그리트 미센든에 묻혔다.

mg.Montgomery: Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

pt.lifeSummary: John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick nasceu em Hook Norton, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra filho de Guillaume Vervolière e N. Ele casou-se com Christian Sanford em 1234, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1223. Em 1241, sua profissão aparece como guardian of heiresses em Coombe Bissett, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 26 de fevereiro de 1263, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Great Missenden, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick родился в местности хук нортон, оксфордшир, Англия. Его родителей звали Guillaume Vervolière и N. Он женился на женщине по имени Christian Sanford в 1234 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. В 1223 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1241 годуработал по профессии guardian of heiresses в местности кумб биссетт, уилцхир, Англия. Он умер 26 февраля 1263 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности грит миссенден, буккингхамшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick nació en Hook Norton, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra como hijo de Guillaume Vervolière y N. Se casó con Christian Sanford en 1234, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1223. En 1241, su ocupación registrada era guardian of heiresses en Coombe Bissett, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Murió el 26 de febrero de 1263, en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Great Missenden, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra.
